description:
Today SKILL.md explains that 'gates' blocks close of the parent until the gate target is closed. What it doesn't say: when should an agent actually claim and start working on a gate task?

Problem: an agent running 'bl ready' sees gate-target tasks in the queue alongside everything else. If they claim the audit task before the parent has reached 'review', they are auditing an artifact that doesn't exist yet — the work will be wasted or redone once the parent lands.

Fix (docs): add guidance to SKILL.md that says roughly:

  If a task is the target of a 'gates' link from a parent (i.e. some other task has 'gates <this>'), don't claim it until the parent is at status=review or later. The artifact being audited doesn't exist until then. Gate tasks exist to validate a near-final product, not to run in parallel with construction.

  Check via: bl show <gate-target-id> then inspect the links pointing at it, or bl show <parent> to see the parent's status.

Placement: in the 'Dependencies and Links' section, right after the link-types table, or as its own short subsection since the guidance is non-obvious.

Open question (not in scope for this ball, file a separate one if desired): should 'bl ready' enforce this automatically by hiding gate-targets whose source parent has status open|in_progress? Argument for: agents don't have to know the rule. Argument against: being explicit in docs keeps bl ready's semantics simple (ready = deps closed), and sometimes you want to start the audit early (e.g. security review of design before impl).

Acceptance:
- SKILL.md has the guidance in a findable place.
- Example wording that an agent parsing the doc would correctly interpret (i.e. no weasel words).
title: SKILL.md: guide agents to not tackle gates until parent is in review
